title: Pan Seared Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: Grassy and flaky Malaysian dish featuring caramelized sugar sauce, perfect for a special occasion, this kosher friendly recipe is sure to impress.
instructions: Prepare the marinade by combining coconut milk, vegetable oil, sugar, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, and ginger in a large bowl.
Add the chicken strips to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium high heat and thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Grill the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the caramelized sugar sauce by heating 2 tablespoons of sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Once the sugar has dissolved, increase the heat to medium high and bring the sauce to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve the grilled chicken satay with the caramelized sugar sauce for dipping.
Garnish with sliced lime and chopped cilantro, if desired.
